title: Save Current Presentation to Public Folder
description: Save a copy of the frontmost presentation to the current user’s Public folder.

title: Export All Images
description: Create and open a folder in the Pictures folder containing all of the images from the frontmost document. NOTE: the document must be in single-file (archive) format.

title: AirDrop Frontmost Presentation
description: Begin an AirDrop session for the current presentation. NOTE: The presentation must have beensaved prior to using this command.

phrases: save [this] presentation to [my] thumb drive and eject [it]
copy [this] presentation to [my] thumb drive and eject [it]
title: Copy Presentation: To Thumb Drive and Eject
description: Copy the frontmost presentation to the mounted thumb drive and eject after the copy has completed.

title: Export Presentation to PDF File
description: Export the frontmost presentation to a PDF file in the Documents folder, and reveal the new file in the Finder.

title: Export Presentation to Movie File
description: Export the frontmost presentation to a movie file in the Movies folder, and reveal the new file in the Finder.

title: Capture Current Slide to Public Folder
description: Save a screen capture of the current slide to the current user’s Public folder.

title: Select Default Title Box
description: Selects the slide’s default title item. To edit a default title item, use the command: “edit slide title”

title: Duplicate Current Slide for Animation Sequence
description: Duplicates the current slide in place, with the duplicate following the original slide. In addition, a Magic Move transition is applied to the original slide.

title: Turn Slide Numbers Off
description: Hides the display of slide numbers throughout the presentation.

phrases: make [a] panoramic sequence with this slide
make [a] panoramic sequence using this slide
make [a] panoramic sequence for this slide
create [a] panoramic sequence with this slide
create [a] panoramic sequence using this slide
create [a] panoramic sequence for this slide
title: Create Long Panoramic Sequence
description: Creates a 4-slide panoramic sequence using the image on the current slide. The image is panned left-to-right for 20-seconds. Note: the current slide must contain only a single panoramic image (pano).

title: DIctate Slide Title
description: Replaces the content of the current slide’s default title item with the text cursor, ready to accept dictation.

title: Turn Slide Title On
description: Shows the current slide’s default title item.

phrases: make [a] long panoramic sequence with this slide
make [a] long panoramic sequence using this slide
make [a] long panoramic sequence for this slide
make [a] long pano sequence with this slide
make [a] long pano sequence using this slide
make [a] long pano sequence for this slide
title: Create Panoramic Sequence
description: Creates a 4-slide panoramic sequence using the image on the current slide. The image is panned left-to-right for 10-seconds. Note: the current slide must contain only a single panoramic image (pano).

title: New Presentation from Current Slide
description: Creates a new presentation file that contains a single slide copy of the current slide.

title: Turn Slide Title Off
description: Hides the current slide’s default title item.

title: Turn Slide Numbers On
description: Shows the display of slide numbers throughout the presentation.

title: Stop Edit
description: Stop the current text edit. Equivalent to pressing the Escape key.

title: Turn Slide Body On
description: Shows the current slide’s default body item.

title: Identify Image Stack
description: Shows the stacking order of the images of the current slide by placing a number over each in turn, and speaking the stack index. Image 1 is always the topmost slide in the stack of images.

title: Select Default Body Box
description: Selects the slide’s default body item. To edit a default body item, use the command: “edit slide body”

title: Move Current Slide to End
description: Move the current slide to the end of all slides.

title: Insert Slide
description: Inserts a default slide after the current slide.

title: DIctate Slide Body
description: Replaces the content of the current slide’s default body item with the text cursor, ready to accept dictation.

title: Insert Blank Slide
description: Inserts a new blank slide after the current slide.

title: Turn Slide Body Off
description: Hides the current slide’s default body item.

title: Duplicate Current Slide
description: Duplicates the current slide in place, with the duplicate following the original slide.

title: Append Blank Slide
description: Adds a new blank slide to the end of the presentation.

phrases: what is [the] number of this slide
title: Speak Slide Number
description: This command will speak the slide number of the current slide

title: Play Presentation
description: This command will start playing the frontmost presentation from the current slide

title: Play Presentation from the Beginning
description: This command will start playing the frontmost presentation from the first un-skipped slide

title: Stop the Presentation
description: This command will stop the currently playing presentation.

title: Save Presentation: To Document Folder with Auto-Naming
description: Save the frontmost presentation to the Documents folder using the title of the first slide as the filename. If the frontmost presentation has already been saved, then save to existing file.

title: Layout View: Scale to Slide
description: Scale the layout view to fit the current slide

phrases: close every presentation and don't save [them]
close every one of the presentations without saving [it]
close every one of these presentations without saving [it]
close all [of] the presentations without saving [them]
close all [of] these presentations without saving [them]
title: Close Presentation: All-No Save
description: Close all open presentations without saving them

title: Layout View: Scale to Selected Items
description: Scale the layout view (up to 400%) to fit the selected slide items

phrases: close [this] presentation and don't save it
close [the] front presentation and don't save it
title: Close Presentation: Front-No Save
description: Close the frontmost presentation without saving it

title: Update Selected Image with Photos Master
description: Update the selected image with a new export of its related master image from the current Photos library. NOTE: the selected image must be in the current Photos library, and have been imported into the presentation using Dictation Commands.

title: Edit Description of Selected Image
description: This commands displays an editing dialog containing the description of the selected image. If there is only one image on the slide, it will be the target of the command, selected or not.

title: Choose File for Sixth Image of Current Slide
description: Using the standard system chooser dialog, select and import an image file for the sixth image of the current slide

title: Choose Files for All Images on Current Slide
description: Using the standard system chooser dialog, select and import an image file for each of the images on the current slide

phrases: scale this [image] to fit [the] slide height
size this [image] to fit [the] slide height
scale this [image] to match [the] slide height
size this [image] to match [the] slide height
title: Scale Image to Fit Slide Height
description: Scale the selected image to fit the slide height and then center the scaled image horizontally.

title: Describe Largest Image
description: Speaks the description of the largest image (by volume) on the current slide.

title: Show Selected Image in Photos
description: Show the selected image in the Maps application. NOTE: the selected image must be in the current Photos library, and have been imported into the presentation using Dictation Commands.

title: Edit Selected Image in Photos
description: Edit the selected image in the Photos application. NOTE: the selected image must be in the current Photos library, and have been imported into the presentation using Dictation Commands.

phrases: add [a] QR code for [the] location of this image
add [a] QR code for [the] location of this photo
title: Overlay Location QR Code
description: Overlay a QR code containing a Google Maps link to the location of the selected image.

title: Choose Files for the Selected Images
description: Using the standard system chooser dialog, select and import an image file for each of the selected images. If no images are selected, then all images on the slide will be processed.

title: Show Selected Image in Photos
description: Show the selected image (full-window) in the Photos application. NOTE: the selected image must be in the current Photos library, and have been imported into the presentation using Dictation Commands.

phrases: put titles on top of every image
put names on top of every image
title: Overlay Image Titles
description: Place the title (name) of each image on top of it as a text overlay. If the image was previously imported into Keynote from Photos using Dictation Commands, this command will attempt to locate the image in the current Photos library and use the title assigned to it.

phrases: turn this into a QR code
make a QR code from this
title: Add a QR code using contents of selected text item
description: This command will convert the contents of the selected text item into a QR code and add it to the current slide. NOTE: select the text item, not its contents.

phrases: scale this [image] to fit [the] slide width
scale this [image] to fit [the] width [of the slide]
size this [image] to fit [the] slide width
size this [image] to fit [the] width [of the slide]
scale this [image] to match [the] slide width
scale this [image] to match [the] width [of the slide]
size this [image] to match [the] slide width
size this [image] to match [the] width [of the slide]
title: Scale Image to Fit Slide Width
description: Scale the selected image to fit the slide width and then center the scaled image vertically.

title: Import Selected Finder Item
description:
Import the currently selected image file in the Finder into the selected image frame in Keynote.

If no image is selected in Keynote, and the slide contains a single image, it will be the recipeient of the import.

If the current slide contains multiple images, the selected image will be the recipient of the import.

If the slide has no images, the Finder item will be imported into the slide and automatically sized.

NOTE: Imported image files will include any embedded description metadata. Also, movie files are compatible for import and will be replace the contents of a target Keynote image frame.

title: Choose File for Third Image of Current Slide
description: Using the standard system chooser dialog, select and import an image file for the third image of the current slide

title: Choose File for First Image of Current Slide
description: Using the standard system chooser dialog, select and import an image file for the first image of the current slide

title: Add Chosen Placeholder Image
description: Add a placeholder image of a chosen porportion and size to the current slide.

title: Import Selected Photos Item
description:
Import the currently selected photo in Photos into the selected image in Keynote.

If no image is selected in Keynote, and the slide contains a single image, it will be the recipeient of the import.

If the current slide contains multiple images, the selected image will be the recipient of the import.

If the slide has no images, the exported Photos photo will be imported into the slide and automatically sized.

NOTE: imported images are automatically “linkable” back to Photos, and will include any description from Photos.

title: Read the Slide
description:
This Dictation Command will speak the slide’s title, body, and image descriptions. Missing items are ignored. Also, the contents of text items will be read if no title or body is showing.

NOTE: specific sounds are played before each reading indicating the type of element whose contents are about to be read. The higher sound (F/C3) indicates an image description is about to be read; the middle sound (D/C3) indicates the title is about to be read; and the lower sound (G/C2) indicates that the body is about to be read. If the contents of text items are read (see above), a secondary middle tone (C/C3) will be sounded.
